title: Report: LeBron James ‘hates’ Celtics, would rank them last on his list of teams to join
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: LeBron James reportedly ranks Celtics as the least likely team he'd join during free agency.
ESPN's Brian Windhorst states James 'hates' Boston, ranking Celtics 30th on his list.
James considering Heat, Cavaliers, and Warriors for potential final team switch.

title: Tom Brady busy at Fanatics Fest, from a face slap to showing off his arm
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Tom Brady slapped Logan Paul during a panel at Fanatics Fest in New York City on July 17, 2026.
Brady and Paul had an ongoing feud since Paul's team defeated Brady's in a flag football tournament in March.
Brady also showcased his athleticism at Fanatics Fest by throwing a baseball with accuracy and engaging in other sports activities.

title: 4 charged in connection with alleged kidnapping, beating near Nantucket park
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Four Nantucket men charged with kidnapping and assault after a 28-year-old man was allegedly beaten and tied with an extension cord.
Suspects identified as Calray Brown, Dijon Hazel, Newton Lawrence, and Alvin Barnett, allegedly attacked the victim near Winter Park.
Victim treated for non-life-threatening injuries; suspects released on bail and scheduled for arraignment on Monday.

title: 2 arrested at Logan Airport as ICE activity ramps up
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Maria Rosales and David Ardila were arrested by ICE at Logan Airport while trying to board flights home.
Rosales was detained due to a 2017 removal order despite pending DACA renewal; Ardila for allegedly overstaying his visa.
Their attorney claims ICE made about 10 immigration-related arrests at Logan Airport in the past three months.

title: 5 movies to watch after ‘The Odyssey’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey' inspires a curated list of similar thematic films.
'Interstellar', 'Lawrence of Arabia', and 'O Brother, Where Art Thou?' highlight direct and stylistic parallels to 'The Odyssey'.
Films range from sci-fi and historical epics to comedic and animated takes on Homer's classic journey.

title: Formidable Red Sox extend win streak to 11 games with doubleheader sweep of Rays
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox extend their win streak to 11 games by sweeping the Tampa Bay Rays in a doubleheader.
Jarren Duran and Carlos Narvaez led with key hits as Boston won Game 1 with a 10-0 victory.
Boston's bullpen was dominant in Game 2, securing a 5-3 win over the Rays, pushing their record to 48-48.

title: Chad Tracy offers updates on several injured Red Sox pitchers
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox interim manager Chad Tracy reports progress for injured pitchers Ranger Suarez and Connelly Early.
Kutter Crawford, Tanner Houck, and Johan Oviedo are advancing in their injury recoveries, aiming for season returns.
Injured players Roman Anthony, Garrett Crochet, and others have no updates or timetables for their returns.

title: 4 Red Sox who rose their stock following Friday’s doubleheader sweep vs. Rays
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Masataka Yoshida drove Boston's success with three hits, including a home run, in the first game against the Rays.
Caleb Durbin batted 5 for 7, scoring two runs, boosting his season performance after a slow start.
Jake Bennett pitched 6 scoreless innings with one hit allowed, helping conserve the bullpen for Game 2.

title: Robert Kraft backs Mike Vrabel after controversial offseason: ‘No one is infallible’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots owner Robert Kraft expressed support for head coach Mike Vrabel after a controversial offseason on ESPN's First Take.
Vrabel denied an affair with NFL reporter Dianna Russini, who later resigned from The Athletic after an investigation.
Vrabel led the Patriots to the Super Bowl in his first season as head coach, despite losing to Seattle.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Anya Taylor-Joy stars in 'Lucky', a crime series streaming now on Apple TV.
'Obsession' grossed over $400 million on a $750,000 budget and is streaming on Peacock.
'The Hunt for the Wilderpeople', starring the late Sam Neill, is available on Prime Video and Plex.
